title: Law of Cosines
text: How could we use the law of cosines to find the length of the third side? Remember that the law of cosines is $$c^2=a^2+b^2+2\operatorname{abcos}\left(X\right)$$, X is the given angle between a and $$b$$ and $$cos(X)=\frac{a^2+b^2-c^2}{2ab}$$

title: Side
text: Which side length are we solving for?

title: Missing angle
text: What angle are we missing to be able to use the cosine law? How do we find this angle given that we know $$2$$ side lengths and have another angle given to us? Consider the law of sines for this.

title: Area
text: How can we find the area after we have found the $$3$$ side lengths?
